from cadquery import Vector

def generate_model():
    # Define dimensions (parametric)
    diameter_large = 10.0
    diameter_small = 6.0
    length_total = 25.0
    length_middle = 8.0
    gap_length = 3.0
    
    # Create the profile in XY plane
    profile = cq.Workplane(cq.Plane())
    
    # Start at origin, go right to center of large circle
    profile.moveTo(0, -diameter_large/2)
    profile.lineTo(diameter_large, -diameter_large/2)
    profile.arc(-diameter_large/2, 0, 90, 180)
    profile.arc(0, 0, 180, 270)
    profile.arc(diameter_large/2, 0, 270, 360)
    
    # Add middle section
    profile.moveTo(diameter_large/2 + gap_length, 0)
    profile.lineTo(diameter_large/2 + gap_length + diameter_small, 0)
    profile.arc(diameter_large/2 + gap_length + diameter_small, 0, 90, 180)
    profile.arc(diameter_large/2 + gap_length + diameter_small, 0, 180, 270)
    profile.arc(diameter_large/2 + gap_length + diameter_small, 0, 270, 360)
    
    # Extrude the profile to create the base shape
    base = profile.extrude(length_total)
    
    # Create fillets for smooth transitions
    result = base.faces().filter(lambda f: f.normalAt().length > 0).val()
    final_shape = base.filletEdges(1.0)
    
    return final_shape

result = generate_model()

import cadquery as cq
from cadquery import Vector

def generate_model():
    # Define parameters for the puzzle piece shape
    length = 100.0
    width = 80.0
    height = 20.0
    notch_width = 25.0
    notch_depth = 15.0
    
    # Create a base rectangle
    base = cq.Workplane(cq.Plane())
    base = base.rect(length, width)
    
    # Extrude to create the main body
    body = base.extrude(height)
    
    # Create notches on each side
    # Top notch (negative Y direction)
    top_notch = body.faces().filter(lambda v: abs(v.normalAt().y) > 0.9).next()
    top_cut = cq.Workplane(top_notch.plane())
    top_cut = top_cut.moveTo(0, 0).lineTo(notch_width, 0).lineTo(notch_width, -notch_depth).lineTo(0, -notch_depth).close()
    top_cut = top_cut.extrude(-notch_depth)
    body = body.cut(top_cut)
    
    # Bottom notch (positive Y direction)
    bottom_notch = body.faces().filter(lambda v: abs(v.normalAt().y) < -0.9).next()
    bottom_cut = cq.Workplane(bottom_notch.plane())
    bottom_cut = bottom_cut.moveTo(0, 0).lineTo(notch_width, 0).lineTo(notch_width, notch_depth).lineTo(0, notch_depth).close()
    bottom_cut = bottom_cut.extrude(notch_depth)
    body = body.cut(bottom_cut)
    
    # Left notch (negative X direction)
    left_notch = body.faces().filter(lambda v: abs(v.normalAt().x) > 0.9).next()
    left_cut = cq.Workplane(left_notch.plane())
    left_cut = left_cut.moveTo(0, 0).lineTo(notch_width, 0).lineTo(notch_width, -notch_depth).lineTo(0, -notch_depth).close()
    left_cut = left_cut.extrude(-notch_depth)
    body = body.cut(left_cut)
    
    # Right notch (positive X direction)
    right_notch = body.faces().filter(lambda v: abs(v.normalAt().x) < -0.9).next()
    right_cut = cq.Workplane(right_notch.plane())
    right_cut = right_cut.moveTo(0, 0).lineTo(notch_width, 0).lineTo(notch_width, notch_depth).lineTo(0, notch_depth).close()
    right_cut = right_cut.extrude(notch_depth)
    body = body.cut(right_cut)
    
    return body

result = generate_model()

# Define parameters for the object
length = 300.0
width = 20.0

hole_diameter = 5.0
hole_spacing = 60.0

base_block = (cq.Workplane()
    .moveTo(0, 0)
    .lineTo(length, 0)
    .lineTo(length, height)
    .lineTo(0, height)
    .close()
    .extrude(width))

# Create holes at specified positions along the length
holes = []
for i in range(2):
    hole_x = i * hole_spacing + (hole_spacing / 2)
    hole_y = height / 2

# Combine all parts into one solid
result = holes[0]
for i in range(1, len(holes)):
    result = result.union(holes[i])

# Finalize the model

import cadquery as cq
from cadquery import Vector

def generate_model():
    # Define parametric dimensions
    thickness = 10.0
    height = 60.0
    length = 200.0
    hole_diameter = 15.0
    stop_length = 30.0
    stop_width = 20.0
    stop_height = 25.0
    chamfer_size = 2.0
    
    # Start with a base block (the main body)
    base_block = (cq.Workplane()
        .moveTo(0, 0)
        .lineTo(length, 0)
        .lineTo(length, height)
        .lineTo(0, height)
        .close()
        .extrude(thickness))
    
    # Add the circular hole on the front face
    hole = (cq.Workplane()
        .moveTo(0, 0)
        .circle(hole_diameter / 2)
        .extrude(-thickness))
    
    # Combine base block and hole using union
    combined = base_block.union(hole)
    
    # Create the stop feature at the end
    stop = (cq.Workplane()
        .moveTo(0, 0)
        .lineTo(stop_length, 0)
        .lineTo(stop_length, stop_width)
        .lineTo(0, stop_width)
        .close()
        .extrude(stop_height))
    
    # Position the stop at the far end of the part
    stop_positioned = stop.translate((length - stop_length/2, 0, 0))
    
    # Union the stop with the combined part
    final_part = combined.union(stop_positioned)
    
    # Apply chamfers to the top edge where the stop meets the main body
    chamfered = final_part.faces().filter(lambda f: abs(f.Center().X - length + stop_length/2) < 0.01).val()[0]
    chamfered_with_chamfer = (cq.Workplane()
        .add(final_part)
        .faces()
        .filter(lambda f: abs(f.Center().X - length + stop_length/2) < 0.01)
        .chamfer(chamfer_size, 45))
    
    result = chamfered_with_chamfer
    
    return result

result = generate_model()

from cadquery import Vector

# Define parametric dimensions for the skate ramp
width = 1.0       # Width of the ramp (Z-axis)
length = 2.5      # Total length of the ramp (X-axis)
height = 0.8      # Maximum height of the ramp (Y-axis)
landing_width = 1.2   # Width of the flat landing area at the top
landing_length = 0.6  # Length of the flat landing area along X

# Create a profile sketch in the XY plane (side view)
profile_sketch = cq.Workplane(cq.Plane())
profile_sketch.moveTo(0, 0)                  # Start bottom-left corner
profile_sketch.lineTo(length - landing_length, 0)  # Bottom edge to start of landing
profile_sketch.lineTo(length - landing_length, height)  # Up to top of landing
profile_sketch.lineTo(0, height)             # Top edge of landing
profile_sketch.close()

# Extrude the side profile to create a solid block with curved surface
solid_block = profile_sketch.extrude(width)

# Now we need to add the rounded/curved transition. We'll do this by:
# 1. Creating a separate curved face and sweeping it, or
# 2. Using a loft between two profiles.
# Let's use a simpler approach: create a base block and then add a rounded wedge.

# Create a base rectangular prism that matches the overall bounding box
base_box = cq.Workplane(cq.Plane()).box(length, width, height)

# Create a rounded wedge shape using a swept feature
# Profile 1: The curved front face (in YZ plane)
curve_profile = cq.Workplane(cq.Plane(cq.Vector(0, 0, 0), cq.Vector(0, 0, 1), cq.Vector(-1, 0, 0)))
curve_profile.moveTo(0, 0)
curve_profile.lineTo(length - landing_length, 0)
curve_profile.lineTo(length - landing_length, height)
curve_profile.lineTo(0, height)
curve_profile.circle(height / 4)  # Add a small radius at the top for smoothness
curve_profile.close()

# Sweep the curve profile along the Z axis (width)
curved_wedge = curve_profile.sweep(width)

# Combine the base block and the curved wedge using boolean union
result = solid_block.union(curved_wedge)

import cadquery as cq
from cadquery import Vector

def generate_model():
    # Define parametric dimensions for the bar
    length = 100.0
    width = 20.0
    height = 10.0
    
    # Create the main rectangular block
    block = cq.Workplane(cq.Plane())
    block = block.moveTo(0, 0).lineTo(length, 0).lineTo(length, width).lineTo(0, width).close()
    block = block.extrude(height)
    
    # Calculate hole positions (centered along the length, spaced apart)
    hole_diameter = 5.0
    hole_depth = 8.0
    spacing_from_end = 15.0
    
    # First hole position
    hole1_pos = Vector(spacing_from_end, width / 2, height / 2)
    
    # Second hole position (symmetrically placed)
    hole2_pos = Vector(length - spacing_from_end, width / 2, height / 2)
    
    # Create holes using subtract operations
    hole1 = cq.Workplane(cq.Plane(hole1_pos))
    hole1 = hole1.addCircle(hole_diameter / 2, 0, 0)
    hole1 = hole1.extrude(-hole_depth)
    
    hole2 = cq.Workplane(cq.Plane(hole2_pos))
    hole2 = hole2.addCircle(hole_diameter / 2, 0, 0)
    hole2 = hole2.extrude(-hole_depth)
    
    # Combine all parts: start with block, then subtract both holes
    result = block.cut(hole1).cut(hole2)
    
    return result

result = generate_model()

def generate_model():

    width = 100.0
    height = 60.0
    depth = 80.0
    hole_diameter = 20.0
    fillet_radius = 5.0
    chamfer_size = 3.0
    
    # Start with a base block
    result = (cq.Workplane("XY")
        .box(width, depth, height))
    
    # Create the main body with rounded transition
    profile = (cq.Workplane("XY")
        .moveTo(0, 0)
        .lineTo(width/2, 0)
        .arc(width/2 + fillet_radius, 0, -90)
        .lineTo(width/2 + fillet_radius, height)
        .lineTo(0, height)
        .close())
    
    # Extrude the profile to create the main shape
    main_body = profile.extrude(depth)
    
    # Add the circular hole on the right side
    hole_pos_x = width - hole_diameter/2
    hole_pos_y = height/2
    
    result = (main_body
        .workplane(offset=depth/2)
        .circle(hole_diameter/2)
        .cutBlind(hole_diameter))
    
    # Add chamfers to the top corners
    result = (result
        .faces(">Z")
        .chamfer(chamfer_size, chamfer_size))
    
    return result

from cadquery import Vector

def generate_model():
    # Define parameters
    plate_thickness = 10.0
    plate_height = 60.0
    plate_length = 200.0
    cutout_width = 30.0
    cutout_depth = 50.0
    handle_diameter = 40.0
    handle_length = 150.0
    
    # Create the main plate with a rectangular cutout
    plate = (cq.Workplane()
        .moveTo(0, plate_height / 2)
        .lineTo(plate_length, plate_height / 2)
        .lineTo(plate_length, -plate_height / 2)
        .lineTo(0, -plate_height / 2)
        .close()
        .extrude(-plate_thickness))
    
    # Add the cutout on the right side of the plate
    cutout = (cq.Workplane()
        .moveTo(plate_length - cutout_depth, plate_height / 2)
        .lineTo(plate_length, plate_height / 2)
        .lineTo(plate_length, -plate_height / 2)
        .lineTo(plate_length - cutout_depth, -plate_height / 2)
        .close()
        .extrude(plate_thickness))
    
    # Combine the plate and cutout using Boolean difference
    base = plate.cut(cutout)
    
    # Create the cylindrical handle attached to the left end of the plate
    handle = (cq.Workplane()
        .circle(handle_diameter / 2)
        .extrude(handle_length))
    
    # Position the handle at the left end of the plate
    handle = handle.moveZ(-plate_thickness / 2).moveX(-handle_length / 2).moveY(plate_height / 2)
    
    # Combine the base and handle
    result = base.union(handle)
    
    return result

import cadquery as cq
from cadquery import Vector

def generate_model():
    # Define parametric dimensions
    outer_width = 10.0
    outer_height = 5.0
    wall_thickness = 2.0
    length = 60.0
    
    # Create the base solid (outer box)
    base_solid = cq.Workplane(cq.Plane())
    base_solid = base_solid.moveTo(outer_width / 2, outer_height / 2)
    base_solid = base_solid.lineTo(-outer_width / 2, outer_height / 2)
    base_solid = base_solid.lineTo(-outer_width / 2, -outer_height / 2)
    base_solid = base_solid.lineTo(outer_width / 2, -outer_height / 2)
    base_solid = base_solid.close()
    base_solid = base_solid.extrude(length)
    
    # Create the inner void (inner box)
    inner_width = outer_width - 2 * wall_thickness
    inner_height = outer_height - 2 * wall_thickness
    
    inner_solid = cq.Workplane(cq.Plane())
    inner_solid = inner_solid.moveTo(inner_width / 2, inner_height / 2)
    inner_solid = inner_solid.lineTo(-inner_width / 2, inner_height / 2)
    inner_solid = inner_solid.lineTo(-inner_width / 2, -inner_height / 2)
    inner_solid = inner_solid.lineTo(inner_width / 2, -inner_height / 2)
    inner_solid = inner_solid.close()
    inner_solid = inner_solid.extrude(length)
    
    # Subtract the inner void from the outer solid
    result = base_solid.cut(inner_solid)
    
    return result

result = generate_model()

def create_model():
    # Define parameters for the model
    bar_length = 150.0
    bar_width = 20.0

    end_diameter = 24.0
    hole_diameter = 6.0
    fillet_radius = 3.0
    
    # Create the main rectangular bar
    bar = (cq.Workplane("XY")
        .moveTo(0, 0)
        .lineTo(bar_length, 0)
        .threePointArc((bar_length + fillet_radius, 0), (bar_length + fillet_radius, bar_width))
        .lineTo(bar_length - fillet_radius, bar_width)
        .threePointArc((bar_length - fillet_radius, 0), (0, 0))
        .close()
        .extrude(-bar_height))
    
    # Create circular ends with holes
    end1 = (cq.Workplane("XZ")
        .circle(end_diameter / 2)
        .translate((bar_length, 0, 0))
        .circle(hole_diameter / 2))
    
    end2 = (cq.Workplane("XZ")
        .circle(end_diameter / 2)
        .translate((0, 0, 0))
        .circle(hole_diameter / 2))
    
    # Combine all parts
    result = (bar.cut(end1).cut(end2))
    
    return result

# Define parameters for the torus

height = 20.0

profile = cq.Workplane(cq.Plane(cq.Vector(0, 0, 0)))
profile = profile.moveTo(-outer_radius, 0).lineTo(-inner_radius, 0).lineTo(-inner_radius, height).lineTo(-outer_radius, height).close()

ring_segment = profile.extrude(height=inner_radius - outer_radius)

torus = ring_segment.rotate((0, 0, 0), (1, 0, 0), 360)

import cadquery as cq
from cadquery import Vector

def generate_model():
    # Define parametric dimensions
    outer_width = 100.0
    outer_height = 80.0
    base_thickness = 10.0
    post_diameter = 20.0
    post_height = 30.0
    post_inner_diameter = 6.0
    frame_width = 15.0
    
    # Create the main rectangular base with a hole in the center
    base = (cq.Workplane("XY")
        .rect(outer_width, outer_height)
        .extrude(base_thickness))
    
    # Create the four posts at the corners
    # Calculate corner positions
    x_pos = outer_width / 2 - frame_width / 2
    y_pos = outer_height / 2 - frame_width / 2
    
    post1 = (cq.Workplane("XY")
        .circle(post_diameter / 2)
        .moveTo(x_pos, y_pos)
        .extrude(post_height))
    
    post2 = (cq.Workplane("XY")
        .circle(post_diameter / 2)
        .moveTo(outer_width - x_pos, y_pos)
        .extrude(post_height))
    
    post3 = (cq.Workplane("XY")
        .circle(post_diameter / 2)
        .moveTo(x_pos, outer_height - y_pos)
        .extrude(post_height))
    
    post4 = (cq.Workplane("XY")
        .circle(post_diameter / 2)
        .moveTo(outer_width - x_pos, outer_height - y_pos)
        .extrude(post_height))
    
    # Combine all parts
    result = (base
        .union(post1)
        .union(post2)
        .union(post3)
        .union(post4))
    
    # Add holes to the posts
    result = (result
        .workplane()
        .moveTo(x_pos + frame_width/2 - post_inner_diameter/2, y_pos + frame_width/2 - post_inner_diameter/2)
        .circle(post_inner_diameter/2)
        .throughAll()
        .cutBlind())
    
    return result

result = generate_model()

def generate_model():
    # Define parameters for the model
    thickness = 10.0
    width = 50.0

    cut_depth = 15.0
    cut_offset_x = 25.0

    base_block = (cq.Workplane(cq.Plane())
        .moveTo(0, 0)
        .lineTo(width, 0)
        .lineTo(width, thickness)
        .lineTo(0, thickness)
        .close()
        .extrude(height))

    cutout = (cq.Workplane(base_block.faces().filter(lambda f: f.normal == cq.Vector(0, 0, -1)).first())
        .moveTo(cut_offset_x, cut_offset_y)
        .lineTo(cut_offset_x + cut_width, cut_offset_y)
        .lineTo(cut_offset_x + cut_width, cut_offset_y + cut_depth)
        .lineTo(cut_offset_x, cut_offset_y + cut_depth)
        .close()
        .extrude(-thickness))
    
    # Combine the base block and the cutout
    result = base_block.cut(cutout)

from cadquery import Vector

def generate_model():
    # Define dimensions
    length = 100.0
    width = 30.0
    height = 25.0
    hole_diameter = 15.0
    fillet_radius = 8.0
    chamfer_angle = 45.0
    
    # Create the base rectangular block
    base = cq.Workplane(cq.Plane())
    base = base.moveTo(0, 0).lineTo(length, 0).lineTo(length, width).lineTo(0, width).close()
    
    # Extrude the base block
    base_solid = base.extrude(height)
    
    # Create the circular cutout for the hole
    hole = cq.Workplane(cq.Plane(base_solid.faces().sortByArea(mag=-1)[0].center()))
    hole = hole.circle(hole_diameter / 2).extrude(-height)
    
    # Combine the base with the hole
    model = base_solid.cut(hole)
    
    # Apply a fillet to the top edge where the hole is located
    model = model.fillet(fillet_radius)
    
    # Apply a chamfer to one of the long edges
    model = model.chamfer([length/2, 0, 0], [0, 0, height], chamferAngle=chamfer_angle)
    
    return model

result = generate_model()

thickness = 2.0 # Thickness of the walls

# Calculate the center point of the profile to rotate around
center = profile.center()

# Rotate the profile to align with the desired diagonal orientation
rotated_profile = profile.rotate(
    center, 
    [0, 0, 1],  # Axis of rotation (Z-axis)
    -45.0       # Rotation angle (-45 degrees for diagonal alignment)
)

channel = rotated_profile.extrude(thickness)

import cadquery as cq
from cadquery import Vector

# Define parametric dimensions for the model
length = 10.0  # Length of the main rectangular part
width = 8.0    # Width (depth) of the main rectangular part
height = 6.0   # Height of the main rectangular part
radius = 3.0   # Radius of the curved section
curved_section_width = 4.0  # Width of the curved protrusion

# Create the base profile in the XZ plane (top view)
profile = cq.Workplane(cq.Plane(cq.Vector(0, 0, 0)))
profile.moveTo(0, 0) \
    .lineTo(length, 0) \
    .lineTo(length, height) \
    .lineTo(0, height) \
    .close() \
    .extrude(-width)  # Extrude into negative Y direction to create the main block

# Add the curved protrusion on top
# We'll use a rounded rectangle approach by creating a separate face and lofting or using fillet
# Since CadQuery doesn't have direct "rounded rectangle" extrusion, we'll build it step by step

# Create the top surface with a cutout for the curved section
top_profile = cq.Workplane(cq.Plane(cq.Vector(0, 0, height)))
top_profile.moveTo(0, 0) \
    .lineTo(length, 0) \
    .threePointArc((length - radius, radius), (length, radius)) \
    .lineTo(length, height - radius) \
    .threePointArc((length - radius, height - radius), (length - radius, height)) \
    .lineTo(0, height) \
    .close() \
    .extrude(-width)  # This creates the top surface with the curved cutout

# Now add the curved protrusion itself
# Create a workplane at the bottom of the curved section
curved_plane = cq.Workplane(cq.Plane(cq.Vector(0, 0, height - radius)))
curved_profile = cq.Workplane().moveTo(0, 0) \
    .lineTo(curved_section_width, 0) \
    .circle(radius) \
    .close() \
    .extrude(radius)  # Extrude upwards to form the curved protrusion

# Combine all parts
result = (profile + top_profile + curved_profile).fuse()

from cadquery import Vector

# Define parameters for the chair
leg_width = 0.05
leg_length = 0.6
seat_thickness = 0.03
seat_width = 0.45
seat_depth = 0.4
backrest_height = 0.5
backrest_thickness = 0.05
armrest_height = 0.15
armrest_width = 0.08
frame_thickness = 0.03

# Create the legs
legs = (cq.Workplane()
    .moveTo(0, 0)
    .box(leg_width, leg_width, leg_length)
    .translate((0, 0, leg_length)))

# Create the seat
seat = (cq.Workplane()
    .moveTo(0, 0)
    .box(seat_width, seat_depth, seat_thickness)
    .translate((0, 0, leg_length + seat_thickness/2)))

# Create the backrest
backrest = (cq.Workplane()
    .moveTo(0, 0)
    .box(backrest_thickness, seat_depth, backrest_height)
    .translate((seat_width - backrest_thickness/2, 0, leg_length + seat_thickness)))

# Create the armrests
armrest_left = (cq.Workplane()
    .moveTo(0, 0)
    .box(armrest_width, armrest_height, leg_length)
    .translate((0, 0, leg_length)))

armrest_right = (cq.Workplane()
    .moveTo(0, 0)
    .box(armrest_width, armrest_height, leg_length)
    .translate((seat_width - armrest_width, 0, leg_length)))

# Combine all parts
chair = (legs.union(seat)
         .union(backrest)
         .union(armrest_left)
         .union(armrest_right))

# Final result
result = chair

import cadquery as cq
from cadquery import Vector

def create_model():
    # Define parameters for the model
    outer_radius = 50.0
    inner_radius = 20.0
    height = 30.0
    hole_count = 8
    hole_diameter = 10.0
    hole_spacing = 60.0
    
    # Create the base cylinder with a central hole
    base_cylinder = (cq.Workplane()
        .circle(outer_radius)
        .extrude(height))
    
    # Create the through-hole in the center
    center_hole = (cq.Workplane()
        .moveTo(0, 0)
        .circle(inner_radius)
        .extrude(-height))
    
    # Combine the base and center hole
    combined_shape = base_cylinder.cut(center_hole)
    
    # Calculate the positions of the holes on the circular pattern
    angle_step = 360 / hole_count
    hole_positions = []
    for i in range(hole_count):
        angle = i * angle_step
        x = hole_spacing * (1/2) * (1 + (hole_diameter / outer_radius) * (1 - abs(angle - 90) / 90))
        y = hole_spacing * (1/2) * (1 + (hole_diameter / outer_radius) * (1 - abs(angle - 90) / 90))
        hole_positions.append((x, y))
    
    # Create the holes
    holes = []
    for pos in hole_positions:
        hole = (cq.Workplane()
            .moveTo(pos[0], pos[1])
            .circle(hole_diameter / 2)
            .extrude(-height))
        holes.append(hole)
    
    # Cut all holes from the combined shape
    final_shape = combined_shape.cut(cq.Workplane().union(holes))
    
    return final_shape

result = create_model()

import cadquery as cq
from cadquery import Vector

def generate_model():
    # Define parameters for the model dimensions
    base_length = 200.0
    base_width = 100.0
    base_height = 50.0
    ramp_length = 150.0
    ramp_height = 80.0
    step_depth = 40.0
    step_height = 30.0
    top_width = 60.0
    
    # Create the main body block
    body = cq.Workplane(cq.Plane())
    
    # Start with the bottom base rectangle
    body = body.moveTo(0, 0).lineTo(base_length, 0).lineTo(base_length, base_width).lineTo(0, base_width).close()
    
    # Extrude the base to full height
    body = body.extrude(base_height)
    
    # Now add the ramp feature using a sketch on the top face
    ramp_sketch = body.faces().filter(lambda f: f.normalAt().dot(Vector(0, 0, 1)) > 0).val()[0].workplane()
    
    # Define ramp profile points relative to the base coordinate system
    ramp_points = [
        (0, 0),  # Bottom-left corner of ramp start
        (ramp_length, 0),  # Bottom-right corner of ramp end
        (ramp_length, top_width),  # Top-right corner of ramp end
        (0, top_width)  # Top-left corner of ramp start
    ]
    
    # Create the ramp shape
    ramp_profile = ramp_sketch.moveTo(*ramp_points[0]).lineTo(*ramp_points[1]).lineTo(*ramp_points[2]).lineTo(*ramp_points[3]).close()
    
    # Cut the ramp volume from the base
    ramp_cut = ramp_profile.extrude(-base_height + ramp_height)
    
    # Combine the base and ramp cut
    final_body = body.cut(ramp_cut)
    
    # Add the step feature
    step_sketch = final_body.faces().filter(lambda f: f.normalAt().dot(Vector(0, 0, 1)) < 0).val()[0].workplane()
    
    # Define step profile points
    step_points = [
        (step_depth, 0),  # Bottom-left corner of step
        (step_depth, step_depth),  # Inner corner
        (0, step_depth),  # Outer edge
        (0, 0)  # Return to start
    ]
    
    # Create the step shape
    step_profile = step_sketch.moveTo(*step_points[0]).lineTo(*step_points[1]).lineTo(*step_points[2]).lineTo(*step_points[3]).close()
    
    # Cut the step volume
    step_cut = step_profile.extrude(step_height)
    
    # Final model with both features
    result = final_body.cut(step_cut)
    
    return result

# Generate the model
result = generate_model()

import cadquery as cq
from cadquery import Vector

def generate_model():
    # Define parametric dimensions
    width = 10.0      # Width of the bar (in mm)
    height_left = 100.0   # Height of left vertical leg (in mm)
    height_right = 50.0    # Height of right vertical leg (in mm)
    horizontal_length = 150.0  # Length of top horizontal bar (in mm)
    
    # Create a workplane at origin for base construction
    wp = cq.Workplane(cq.Plane())
    
    # Build the L-shaped profile in XY plane:
    # Start from bottom-left corner, go up to height_left, 
    # then right by horizontal_length, then down to height_right
    
    # Profile path coordinates (relative to starting point):
    start_point = Vector(0, 0, 0)
    p1 = Vector(0, height_left, 0)           # Top of left leg
    p2 = Vector(horizontal_length, height_left, 0)  # End of top bar
    p3 = Vector(horizontal_length, height_right, 0)  # Bottom of right leg
    
    # Construct the rectangular cross-section along this polyline
    # Using extrude with a sweep-like approach via box and boolean operations
    
    # First, create the full bounding box that contains all three segments
    total_height = max(height_left, height_right)
    total_width = horizontal_length + width  # Extra width for right leg overlap
    total_depth = width
    
    # Create main block covering entire footprint
    main_block = cq.Workplane() \
        .box(width, total_height, total_depth) \
        .moveTo(0, 0) \
        .extrude(total_height)
    
    # Now subtract the "missing" part under the shorter leg
    # The missing region is a rectangle of size (horizontal_length x (height_left - height_right))
    # positioned at the right end of the structure
    
    missing_region = cq.Workplane() \
        .box(width, height_left - height_right, total_depth) \
        .moveTo(horizontal_length - width, height_right, 0) \
        .extrude(height_left - height_right)
    
    # Boolean subtraction to get final shape
    result = main_block.cut(missing_region)
    
    return result

result = generate_model()

from cadquery import Vector

def generate_model():
    # Create the base solid block
    result = (cq.Workplane()
        .box(100, 80, 60))
    
    # Create a pocket/void on the top surface
    # Starting from the top face and moving inward to create the cutout
    void_profile = (cq.Workplane()
        .moveTo(30, 0)  # Start at left edge of the cutout
        .lineTo(70, 0)  # Go right for the width of the cutout
        .lineTo(70, 40) # Go down for the depth of the cutout
        .lineTo(30, 40) # Go left to close the profile
        .close())
    
    # Extrude the void profile downward to create the pocket
    result = result.cut(void_profile.extrude(-40))
    
    return result

from cadquery import Vector

def generate_model():
    # Define parameters for the model
    length = 200.0
    width = 50.0
    height = 30.0
    cutout_width = 40.0
    cutout_height = 15.0
    cutout_radius = 10.0
    notch_depth = 10.0
    
    # Create the main block with a cutout
    main_block = (cq.Workplane(cq.Plane())
        .box(length, width, height)
        .faces(">Z")
        .workplane()
        .rect(-cutout_width/2, -cutout_height/2, cutout_width, cutout_height)
        .extrude(-cutout_height))
    
    # Create the notch at the end
    notch = (main_block.faces(">Z").workplane().circle(notch_depth).extrude(-notch_depth))
    
    # Combine the main block and notch
    result = main_block.union(notch)
    
    return result

result = generate_model()

import cadquery as cq
from cadquery import Vector

# Define parameters for the model
thickness = 10.0  # Thickness of the plate
width = 100.0     # Width of the rectangular opening
height = 60.0     # Height of the rectangular opening
outer_length = 120.0  # Length of the outer rectangle (along Z)
corner_radius = 5.0   # Radius of the rounded corners on the opening

# Create a Workplane at the origin
wp = cq.Workplane(cq.Plane())

# Start defining the profile in the XY plane
profile = wp.moveTo(0, thickness / 2).lineTo(outer_length, thickness / 2).lineTo(outer_length, -thickness / 2).lineTo(0, -thickness / 2).close()

# Extrude the profile to create the base solid
base_solid = profile.extrude(thickness)

# Now, we need to cut out the rectangular hole with rounded corners.
# We can do this by creating a separate Workplane for the hole and subtracting it.
# The hole is centered within the base.

# Define the hole's position relative to the center of the base
hole_x_offset = outer_length / 2 - width / 2
hole_y_offset = thickness / 2 - height / 2

# Create a new Workplane at the top face of the base to define the hole
hole_wp = base_solid.faces().filter(lambda f: f.normalAt().dot(cq.Vector(0, 0, 1)) > 0).val()[0].workplane()

# Move the hole workplane to the correct location
hole_wp = hole_wp.moveTo(hole_x_offset, hole_y_offset)

# Draw the inner rectangle with rounded corners
inner_rect = hole_wp.moveTo(-width / 2 + corner_radius, -height / 2 + corner_radius).lineTo(width / 2 - corner_radius, -height / 2 + corner_radius).threePointArc((width / 2 - corner_radius, -height / 2), (width / 2 - corner_radius, height / 2 - corner_radius)).lineTo(-width / 2 + corner_radius, height / 2 - corner_radius).threePointArc((-width / 2 + corner_radius, height / 2), (-width / 2 + corner_radius, -height / 2)).close()

# Cut the hole through the entire thickness
hole_cut = inner_rect.extrude(thickness)

# Subtract the hole from the base solid
result = base_solid.cut(hole_cut)

import cadquery as cq
from cadquery import Vector

def generate_model():
    # Define parametric dimensions
    main_cylinder_radius = 30.0
    main_cylinder_height = 100.0
    top_flange_radius = 40.0
    top_flange_thickness = 5.0
    shaft_diameter = 8.0
    shaft_height = 25.0
    
    # Create the main cylinder
    main_cylinder = (cq.Workplane()
        .circle(main_cylinder_radius)
        .extrude(main_cylinder_height))
    
    # Create the top flange by adding a larger cylinder on top
    top_flange = (cq.Workplane()
        .circle(top_flange_radius)
        .extrude(top_flange_thickness))
    
    # Combine the main cylinder and top flange
    combined_shape = main_cylinder.union(top_flange)
    
    # Create the shaft using a smaller cylinder
    shaft = (cq.Workplane()
        .circle(shaft_diameter / 2)
        .extrude(shaft_height, both=True))
    
    # Move the shaft to the top surface of the combined shape
    shaft_positioned = shaft.moved(Vector(0, 0, main_cylinder_height + top_flange_thickness / 2 + shaft_height / 2))
    
    # Union all components together
    final_model = combined_shape.union(shaft_positioned)
    
    return final_model

result = generate_model()

import cadquery as cq
from cadquery import Vector

def generate_model():
    # Define parameters for the model
    length = 200.0
    width = 20.0
    thickness = 10.0
    groove_width = 10.0
    groove_depth = 5.0
    chamfer_size = 2.0
    
    # Create the base block
    base_block = cq.Workplane(cq.Plane())
    base_block.moveTo(0, 0)
    base_block.lineTo(length, 0)
    base_block.lineTo(length, width)
    base_block.lineTo(0, width)
    base_block.close()
    
    base_block = base_block.extrude(thickness)
    
    # Create the grooves
    groove_1 = cq.Workplane(base_block.faces().filter(lambda f: f.normal == cq.Vector(0, 0, -1)).first())
    groove_1.moveTo(0, 0)
    groove_1.lineTo(groove_width, 0)
    groove_1.moveTo(groove_width, 0)
    groove_1.lineTo(groove_width + (length / 3), 0)
    groove_1.close()
    
    groove_1 = groove_1.extrude(-groove_depth)
    
    groove_2 = cq.Workplane(base_block.faces().filter(lambda f: f.normal == cq.Vector(0, 0, -1)).first())
    groove_2.moveTo(length / 3, 0)
    groove_2.lineTo(length / 3 + groove_width, 0)
    groove_2.moveTo(length / 3 + groove_width, 0)
    groove_2.lineTo(length / 3 + groove_width + (length / 3), 0)
    groove_2.close()
    
    groove_2 = groove_2.extrude(-groove_depth)
    
    # Combine the parts
    result = base_block.cut(groove_1).cut(groove_2)
    
    # Apply chamfers to the top edges
    result = result.edges().filter(lambda e: e.tag == "Top").chamfer(chamfer_size)
    
    return result

result = generate_model()

import cadquery as cq
from cadquery import Vector

# Define the parametric dimensions for the flange
outer_diameter = 100.0
inner_diameter = 50.0
flange_thickness = 15.0
bolt_hole_count = 5
bolt_diameter = 12.0
bolt_pattern_radius = 70.0
bolt_depth = 8.0

# Create the main body of the flange (a ring)
body = (cq.Workplane()
    .circle(outer_diameter / 2)
    .extrude(flange_thickness))

# Cut the inner hole to create the central opening
body = (body
    .workplane(offset=-inner_diameter / 2)
    .circle(inner_diameter / 2)
    .cutThruAll())

# Create a list of vectors for the bolt hole positions based on the pattern radius and count
hole_positions = []
for i in range(bolt_hole_count):
    angle = 2 * 3.141592653589793 * i / bolt_hole_count
    x = bolt_pattern_radius * (1 - 2 * (angle < 3.141592653589793)) * ((-1)**i)
    y = bolt_pattern_radius * (1 + 2 * (angle < 3.141592653589793)) * ((-1)**i)
    hole_positions.append(Vector(x, y, 0))

# Add holes at each position
for pos in hole_positions:
    hole = (cq.Workplane()
        .moveTo(pos.x, pos.y)
        .circle(bolt_diameter / 2)
        .extrude(-bolt_depth))
    body = (body
        .workplane(position=Vector(pos.x, pos.y, 0))
        .add(hole))

result = body

import cadquery as cq
from cadquery import Vector

def generate_model():
    # Define dimensions (parametric)
    width = 10.0
    height = 5.0
    length = 100.0
    thickness = 2.0
    
    # Create base block
    base_block = cq.Workplane(cq.Plane())
    base_block = base_block.moveTo(0, 0).lineTo(length, 0).lineTo(length, width).lineTo(0, width).close()
    base_block = base_block.extrude(thickness)
    
    # Add rounded transition on both ends
    def create_rounded_transition(wp, start_pos, end_pos, radius):
        """Create a smooth transition between two positions"""
        direction = (end_pos - start_pos).normalized()
        midpoint = start_pos + direction * (length / 2)
        
        # Create a curve from start to end with rounding
        wp = wp.moveTo(start_pos.x, start_pos.y)
        wp = wp.lineTo(midpoint.x - radius, midpoint.y)
        wp = wp.arc(radius, midpoint.y, radius)
        wp = wp.lineTo(end_pos.x - radius, end_pos.y)
        wp = wp.lineTo(end_pos.x, end_pos.y)
        wp = wp.close()
        return wp.extrude(thickness)
    
    # Apply transitions to both sides
    left_transition = create_rounded_transition(
        cq.Workplane(cq.Plane()),
        Vector(0, 0), 
        Vector(length/4, 0), 
        5.0
    )
    
    right_transition = create_rounded_transition(
        cq.Workplane(cq.Plane()),
        Vector(3*length/4, 0), 
        Vector(length, 0), 
        5.0
    )
    
    # Combine all parts
    result = base_block.fuse(left_transition, glue=True).fuse(right_transition, glue=True)
    
    return result

result = generate_model()

from cadquery import Vector

def generate_model():
    # Define parametric dimensions
    length = 200.0
    width = 30.0
    thickness = 15.0
    shoulder_width = 45.0
    shoulder_length = 30.0
    chamfer_size = 3.0
    
    # Create the base rectangular block with shoulders
    solid = (cq.Workplane()
        .moveTo(0, 0)
        .lineTo(length, 0)
        .lineTo(length, width)
        .lineTo(0, width)
        .close()
        .extrude(thickness))
    
    # Add left shoulder feature
    shoulder_left = (cq.Workplane()
        .moveTo(0, 0)
        .lineTo(shoulder_length, 0)
        .lineTo(shoulder_length, shoulder_width)
        .lineTo(0, shoulder_width)
        .close()
        .extrude(-thickness/2))
    
    # Add right shoulder feature  
    shoulder_right = (cq.Workplane()
        .moveTo(length - shoulder_length, 0)
        .lineTo(length, 0)
        .lineTo(length, shoulder_width)
        .lineTo(length - shoulder_length, shoulder_width)
        .close()
        .extrude(-thickness/2))
    
    # Combine all parts
    result = solid.union(shoulder_left).union(shoulder_right)
    
    return result

result = generate_model()

import cadquery as cq
from cadquery import Vector

def generate_model():
    # Define parametric dimensions
    length = 100.0
    width = 20.0
    height = 10.0
    shoulder_length = 30.0
    groove_width = 8.0
    groove_depth = 2.0
    groove_radius = 2.5
    
    # Create the main block with fillets at shoulders
    body = (cq.Workplane("XY")
        .rect(length, width)
        .extrude(height))
    
    # Add filleted shoulders on both ends
    body = body.add(
        cq.Workplane("XZ")
            .moveTo(shoulder_length/2 + groove_width/2, 0)
            .lineTo(shoulder_length/2 + groove_width/2, -groove_depth)
            .threePointArc(shoulder_length/2 + groove_width/2 + groove_radius, -groove_depth)
            .lineTo(shoulder_length/2 + groove_width/2 + groove_radius, 0)
            .close()
            .extrude(-width)
    )
    
    body = body.add(
        cq.Workplane("XZ")
            .moveTo(length - shoulder_length/2 - groove_width/2, 0)
            .lineTo(length - shoulder_length/2 - groove_width/2, -groove_depth)
            .threePointArc(length - shoulder_length/2 - groove_width/2 - groove_radius, -groove_depth)
            .lineTo(length - shoulder_length/2 - groove_width/2 - groove_radius, 0)
            .close()
            .extrude(-width)
    )
    
    # Cut out the grooves
    body = body.cut(
        cq.Workplane("XY")
            .rect(groove_width, width)
            .translate((length/2 - groove_width/2, 0))
            .extrude(-groove_depth)
    )
    
    result = body
    
    return result

import cadquery as cq
from cadquery import Vector

def generate_model():
    # Define parametric dimensions
    width = 10.0   # Width of the flat face
    height = 20.0  # Length/height of the object
    radius = 5.0   # Radius of the cylindrical arc
    
    # Create base rectangle on XY plane
    rect = (cq.Workplane(cq.Plane())
        .moveTo(-width / 2, -height / 2)
        .lineTo(width / 2, -height / 2)
        .lineTo(width / 2, height / 2)
        .lineTo(-width / 2, height / 2)
        .close())
    
    # Extrude to create initial block
    extruded = rect.extrude(0)  # Thickness will be added later
    
    # Create a circular profile for the curved section
    circle_profile = (cq.Workplane(cq.Plane(cq.Vector(0, 0, 0)))
        .circle(radius))
    
    # Extrude the circle to create a cylinder segment
    cylinder_segment = circle_profile.extrude(height)
    
    # Position the cylinder segment appropriately
    positioned_cylinder = cylinder_segment.translate((0, 0, 0))
    
    # Combine the rectangular and cylindrical parts using boolean union
    combined_shape = extruded.union(positioned_cylinder)
    
    # Apply final transformations if needed
    result = combined_shape
    
    return result

result = generate_model()

import cadquery as cq
from cadquery import Vector

# Define the final geometry for the 3D model
def generate_model():
    # Base dimensions (parametric)
    length = 100.0
    width = 50.0
    height = 40.0
    
    # Create a base rectangular prism
    base_prism = cq.Workplane(cq.Plane())
    
    # Start with a rectangle on XY plane
    base_prism = base_prism.rect(length, width)
    
    # Extrude to create the full block
    base_prism = base_prism.extrude(height)
    
    # Now we need to modify the shape based on the image:
    # The front face appears to be angled inward
    # We'll cut a wedge from one end
    
    # Create a cutting plane at an angle
    # The angle appears to be about 30 degrees from vertical
    angle = 30.0  # degrees
    
    # Calculate the depth of the cut
    cut_depth = length * (1 - 1/(2*(angle/90)))  # Simplified calculation
    
    # Create a wedge-shaped cutter
    wedge = cq.Workplane(cq.Plane())
    wedge = wedge.moveTo(0, 0).lineTo(width, 0).lineTo(width, cut_depth).lineTo(0, cut_depth).close()
    wedge = wedge.extrude(-height)  # Cut in negative Z direction
    
    # Rotate the wedge to match the angle
    wedge = wedge.rotate((0, 0, 0), (length/2, 0, 0), angle)
    
    # Perform the cut operation
    result = base_prism.cut(wedge)
    
    return result

result = generate_model()

import cadquery as cq
from cadquery import Vector

def generate_model():
    # Define parameters for the model
    width = 10.0
    height = 15.0
    depth = 25.0
    cut_width = 4.0
    cut_height = 6.0
    
    # Create the base block
    base = cq.Workplane(cq.Plane())
    base = base.rect(width, depth).extrude(height)
    
    # Create the first cutout (top left)
    cut1 = cq.Workplane(base.faces().filter(lambda f: f.normal == cq.Vector(0, 0, 1))[0].plane())
    cut1 = cut1.moveTo(cut_width/2, 0).lineTo(cut_width/2, cut_height).lineTo(cut_width, cut_height).close()
    cut1 = cut1.extrude(-depth)
    
    # Create the second cutout (bottom right)
    cut2 = cq.Workplane(base.faces().filter(lambda f: f.normal == cq.Vector(0, 0, 1))[0].plane())
    cut2 = cut2.moveTo(0, cut_height + cut_width/2).lineTo(cut_width, cut_height + cut_width/2).lineTo(cut_width, cut_height + cut_width + cut_height/2).close()
    cut2 = cut2.extrude(depth - cut_width)
    
    # Combine all parts
    result = base.cut(cut1).cut(cut2)
    
    return result

result = generate_model()

from cadquery import Vector

def generate_model():
    # Main cylindrical body (left)
    main_cylinder = (cq.Workplane(cq.Plane())
        .circle(30.0)
        .extrude(80.0))
    
    # Hollow center of the cylinder
    hollow_center = (cq.Workplane(cq.Plane())
        .circle(15.0)
        .extrude(80.0, both=True))
    
    # Combine to create a pipe
    pipe = main_cylinder.cut(hollow_center)
    
    # Flat rectangular block on top of the pipe
    flat_block = (cq.Workplane(cq.Plane())
        .moveTo(-20.0, 40.0)
        .lineTo(20.0, 40.0)
        .lineTo(20.0, 60.0)
        .lineTo(-20.0, 60.0)
        .close()
        .extrude(20.0))
    
    # Angled block on the right side
    angled_block = (cq.Workplane(cq.Plane())
        .moveTo(0.0, -30.0)
        .lineTo(60.0, -30.0)
        .lineTo(60.0, 30.0)
        .lineTo(30.0, 60.0)
        .lineTo(-30.0, 60.0)
        .lineTo(-30.0, 30.0)
        .close()
        .extrude(40.0))
    
    # Final assembly by combining all parts
    result = pipe.union(flat_block).union(angled_block)
    
    return result

result = generate_model()

from cadquery import Vector

def generate_model():
    # Define parametric dimensions
    length = 100.0  # Total length of the part
    width = 20.0    # Width of the rectangular section
    thickness = 5.0 # Thickness (height) of the part
    hole_diameter = 8.0   # Diameter of the mounting holes
    corner_radius = 10.0  # Radius for the rounded corners
    
    # Create the base profile on the XY plane
    # We'll construct a rectangle with rounded ends
    base_profile = cq.Workplane(cq.Plane())
    
    # Start at one end, draw half the width up, then along to the center of the straight section
    # Then down, along the other side, and close with the rounded corners
    # Actually, let's do it more directly:
    # Start at bottom-left of the left rounded area
    base_profile.moveTo(-corner_radius, -width/2)
    # Line up to top-left of left rounded area
    .lineTo(-corner_radius, width/2)
    # Arc around the left rounded corner (this will be the start of our main body)
    # But we need to define the full shape first. Let's use a simpler approach.
    
    # Let's build the profile by defining vertices relative to the center line
    # Left end is centered at x = -(length/2 - corner_radius), Right end at x = +(length/2 - corner_radius)
    # The straight section is between these two points.
    
    # Re-strategize: Use the 'box' method for the main body with rounded corners? 
    # No, CadQuery doesn't have a direct 'rounded box'. We must construct the profile.
    
    # Correct Profile Construction:
    # 1. Start at the bottom-left corner of the LEFT rounded end.
    # 2. Go UP to the top-left corner of the LEFT rounded end.
    # 3. ARC rightwards (counter-clockwise if looking from +Z) to the top-right corner of the LEFT rounded end.
    #    Wait, standard arcs in CQ are CCW. So to go from Top-Left to Top-Right on a circle, it's CW.
    #    We can use .arc(center, radius, angle1, angle2)
    
    # Let's try a different construction order that is easier to chain:
    # Start at the BOTTOM-RIGHT of the RIGHT rounded end.
    # Go UP to TOP-RIGHT of RIGHT rounded end.
    # ARC LEFTWARDS (CCW) to BOTTOM-LEFT of RIGHT rounded end.
    # LINE LEFT to BOTTOM-LEFT of LEFT rounded end.
    # LINE DOWN to BOTTOM-RIGHT of LEFT rounded end.
    # ARC LEFTWARDS (CCW) to BOTTOM-LEFT of LEFT rounded end... wait this is getting confusing.
    
    # Simplest way: Construct the "rectangle with semicircular caps" profile.
    # Center of the whole part is (0,0).
    # Length of straight section = length - 2 * corner_radius
    # Half-length of straight section = (length - 2*corner_radius)/2
    
    straight_section_half_len = (length - 2*corner_radius) / 2
    
    # Define the profile starting from the center-bottom point of the straight section
    # Move to (straight_section_half_len, -width/2) -> Bottom-Right of the straight section
    # Line to (straight_section_half_len, width/2) -> Top-Right of the straight section
    # Arc CCW around the right corner: Center=(length/2 - corner_radius, 0), Radius=corner_radius
    # From angle -90 deg (bottom) to +90 deg (top)? No, that would make a D-shape.
    # We want to connect the top-right of the straight section to the top-left of the straight section via an arc.
    # That means going from angle 90 to 180 degrees around the center of the right cap.
    # Center of right cap: (length/2 - corner_radius, 0)
    # Start point (Top-Right of straight): (length/2 - corner_radius, corner_radius) -> Angle 90
    # End point (Bottom-Right of straight): (length/2 - corner_radius, -corner_radius) -> Angle -90 or 270
    # To go CCW from 90 to 270, we sweep 180 degrees. This creates a semi-circle bulging OUT to the right.
    # That's what we want!
    
    # Okay, let's trace the perimeter CCW starting from the origin (center of the straight section, y=-width/2)
    # 1. Move to (straight_section_half_len, -width/2) [Bottom-Right of straight section]
    # 2. Line to (straight_section_half_len, width/2) [Top-Right of straight section]
    # 3. Arc CCW around center (length/2 - corner_radius, 0) from angle 90 to 270 degrees.
    #    This draws the right semi-circular cap.
    # 4. Line to (-straight_section_half_len, width/2) [Top-Left of straight section]
    # 5. Arc CCW around center (-length/2 + corner_radius, 0) from angle 90 to 270 degrees.
    #    This draws the left semi-circular cap.
    # 6. Line to (straight_section_half_len, -width/2) [Back to start]
    
    # Wait, step 4 connects Top-Right of straight to Top-Left of straight. Yes.
    # Step 5 connects Top-Left of straight to Bottom-Left of straight. Yes.
    # Step 6 connects Bottom-Left of straight to Bottom-Right of straight. Yes.
    
    # Let's refine the coordinates:
    # Origin for this profile: Center of the straight section, bottom edge.
    # Point A: (straight_section_half_len, -width/2)
    # Point B: (straight_section_half_len, width/2)
    # Center Right Cap: (length/2 - corner_radius, 0)
    # Center Left Cap: (-length/2 + corner_radius, 0)
    
    # Build the profile
    profile = cq.Workplane(cq.Plane())
    
    # Start at bottom-right of straight section
    profile.moveTo(straight_section_half_len, -width/2)
    # Go up to top-right of straight section
    .lineTo(straight_section_half_len, width/2)
    # Arc around the right corner (CCW)
    .arc(
        center=cq.Vector(length/2 - corner_radius, 0, 0),
        radius=corner_radius,
        angle1=90,
        angle2=270
    )
    # Go to top-left of straight section
    .lineTo(-straight_section_half_len, width/2)
    # Arc around the left corner (CCW)
    .arc(
        center=cq.Vector(-length/2 + corner_radius, 0, 0),
        radius=corner_radius,
        angle1=90,
        angle2=270
    )
    # Close back to start (implicitly handled by the next operation usually, but let's be explicit if needed)
    # Actually, the last arc ends at (-straight_section_half_len, -width/2) which is not the start.
    # My previous logic was slightly flawed. Let's re-trace carefully.
    
    # Revised Trace:
    # Start at (straight_section_half_len, -width/2)
    # Line Up to (straight_section_half_len, width/2)
    # Arc CCW around (length/2 - corner_radius, 0) from 90 to 270 -> Ends at (length/2 - corner_radius, -corner_radius)
    # Line Left to (-straight_section_half_len, -width/2) ? NO.
    # The bottom edge is flat. So after the right arc ends at (length/2 - corner_radius, -corner_radius),
    # I need to go left along the bottom edge to the left arc's bottom start.
    # Left arc starts at its bottom-most point: (-length/2 + corner_radius, -corner_radius).
    # So I need a line from (length/2 - corner_radius, -corner_radius) to (-length/2 + corner_radius, -corner_radius).
    # Then Arc CCW around (-length/2 + corner_radius, 0) from 270 to 90? No, that's CW.
    # From 270 to 90 CCW is 180 degrees. It goes from bottom to top.
    # Then Line Right to (straight_section_half_len, -width/2).
    
    # Let's simplify even further using symmetry.

    #     .moveTo(0, -width/2) # Start at center-bottom of straight section
    #     .lineTo(straight_section_half_len,

import cadquery as cq
from cadquery import Vector

def generate_model():
    # Define base dimensions
    width = 10.0
    depth = 20.0
    height_base = 8.0
    radius_end = 3.0
    
    # Create the main body with rounded ends
    body = (cq.Workplane()
        .moveTo(0, 0)
        .lineTo(width, 0)
        .lineTo(width, depth)
        .lineTo(0, depth)
        .close()
        .extrude(height_base))
    
    # Add the cylindrical feature at one end
    cylinder_pos = Vector(radius_end + 0.5, depth/2, height_base - 2.0)
    cylinder = (cq.Workplane()
        .moveTo(0, 0)
        .circle(radius_end)
        .extrude(-width + 1.0))
    
    # Position and combine with the body
    final_body = body.union(cylinder).translate((radius_end, 0, 0))
    
    # Add the top block structure
    top_block = (cq.Workplane()
        .moveTo(0, 0)
        .lineTo(width - 4.0, 0)
        .lineTo(width - 4.0, 6.0)
        .lineTo(0, 6.0)
        .close()
        .extrude(4.0))
    
    # Position the top block
    positioned_top = top_block.translate((width/2 - 2.0, depth/2, height_base))
    
    # Combine all parts
    result = final_body.union(positioned_top)
    
    return result

result = generate_model()

from cadquery import Vector

def generate_model():
    # Define parametric dimensions
    width = 10.0
    height = 20.0
    thickness = 5.0
    gap = 8.0
    
    # Create the main body with cutouts using a single solid operation
    body = (cq.Workplane()
        .moveTo(0, 0)
        .lineTo(width, 0)
        .lineTo(width, height)
        .lineTo(0, height)
        .close()
        .extrude(thickness))
    
    # Cut out the first notch from the top
    notch1 = (cq.Workplane()
        .moveTo(width/2, 0)
        .lineTo(width - width/4, 0)
        .lineTo(width - width/4, height - gap/2)
        .lineTo(width/2, height - gap/2)
        .close()
        .extrude(-thickness))
    
    # Cut out the second notch from the bottom
    notch2 = (cq.Workplane()
        .moveTo(width/2, height)
        .lineTo(width - width/4, height)
        .lineTo(width - width/4, height + gap/2)
        .lineTo(width/2, height + gap/2)
        .close()
        .extrude(-thickness))
    
    # Combine all operations
    result = body.cut(notch1).cut(notch2)
    
    return result

result = generate_model()

import cadquery as cq
from cadquery import Vector

def create_model():
    # Define parametric dimensions
    outer_radius = 10.0
    inner_radius = 3.5
    height = 8.0
    
    # Create a cylinder with the outer radius and specified height
    solid = cq.Workplane(cq.Plane())
    
    # Start with a circle on the XY plane
    solid = solid.cylinder(outer_radius, height)
    
    # Create a smaller concentric cylinder to form the hole
    solid = solid.cut(cq.Workplane(cq.Plane()).cylinder(inner_radius, height))
    
    # Add fillets to the edges for smooth transitions
    solid = solid.edges().filter(lambda e: e.isCircle()).fillet(2.0)
    
    return solid

import cadquery as cq
from cadquery import Vector

def generate_model():
    # Define parametric dimensions
    L1 = 50.0   # Length of first section
    W1 = 30.0   # Width of first section
    H1 = 20.0   # Height of first section
    
    L2 = 80.0   # Length of second (main) section
    W2 = 40.0   # Width of main section
    H2 = 30.0   # Height of main section
    
    WallThick = 5.0  # Thickness of the tray walls
    Radius = 10.0    # Radius for the curved cutouts
    
    # Create the base block for the first section
    base1 = cq.Workplane(cq.Plane())
    base1.moveTo(0, 0).lineTo(L1, 0).lineTo(L1, -W1).lineTo(0, -W1).close()
    solid1 = base1.extrude(H1)
    
    # Create the base block for the second section
    base2 = cq.Workplane(cq.Plane())
    base2.moveTo(0, 0).lineTo(L2, 0).lineTo(L2, -W2).lineTo(0, -W2).close()
    solid2 = base2.extrude(H2)
    
    # Combine the two base blocks
    combined_base = solid1.union(solid2)
    
    # Create the rectangular slot on top of the second section
    slot_length = L2 - 10.0
    slot_width = W2 - 10.0
    slot_depth = H2 / 2.0
    
    # Create the slot using a workplane at the top face of the second section
    slot_wp = cq.Workplane(solid2.faces().filter(lambda f: abs(f.normalAt().z) > 0.99)[0].val)
    slot_rect = slot_wp.rect(slot_length, slot_width)
    slot_cut = slot_rect.extrude(-slot_depth)
    
    # Cut the slot from the combined base
    with_slot = combined_base.cut(slot_cut)
    
    # Now add the curved cutouts on the sides of the first section
    # We'll create these by cutting cylindrical volumes
    
    # First curved cutout (left side of first section)
    curve1_center = cq.Vector(Radius, -W1/2 + Radius, H1/2)
    curve1_axis = cq.Vector(0, 0, 1)
    curve1_radius = Radius
    
    # Create a cylinder for the first cutout
    cyl1 = cq.Workplane(cq.Plane(curve1_center, curve1_axis))
    cyl1.circle(curve1_radius)
    cyl1_solid = cyl1.extrude(-H1, both=True)
    
    # Second curved cutout (right side of first section)
    curve2_center = cq.Vector(-Radius, -W1/2 + Radius, H1/2)
    curve2_axis = cq.Vector(0, 0, 1)
    curve2_radius = Radius
    
    # Create a cylinder for the second cutout
    cyl2 = cq.Workplane(cq.Plane(curve2_center, curve2_axis))
    cyl2.circle(curve2_radius)
    cyl2_solid = cyl2.extrude(-H1, both=True)
    
    # Apply all cuts to the model
    final_model = with_slot.cut(cyl1_solid).cut(cyl2_solid)
    
    return final_model

result = generate_model()

import cadquery as cq
from cadquery import Vector

def generate_model():
    # Define parametric dimensions
    thickness = 10.0
    width = 40.0
    length = 120.0
    end_radius = 25.0
    hole_diameter = 8.0
    slot_width = 15.0
    slot_length = 70.0
    
    # Create the base profile for extrusion
    # This will be a rounded rectangle with two holes and one slot
    
    profile = (cq.Workplane()
        .moveTo(-width/2, -thickness/2)
        .lineTo(width/2, -thickness/2)
        .threePointArc((width/2 + end_radius, -thickness/2), (width/2 + end_radius, thickness/2))
        .lineTo(width/2 + end_radius, thickness/2)
        .threePointArc((width/2, thickness/2), (width/2, -thickness/2))
        .close()
        .extrude(thickness))
    
    # Add holes at both ends
    holes = (profile
        .workplane(offset=-thickness/2)
        .moveTo(-width/2 + end_radius - hole_diameter/2, 0)
        .circle(hole_diameter/2)
        .cutBlind(length/2 - slot_length/2))
    
    holes = (holes
        .workplane(offset=thickness/2)
        .moveTo(-width/2 + end_radius - hole_diameter/2, 0)
        .circle(hole_diameter/2)
        .cutBlind(length/2 - slot_length/2))
    
    # Add the central slot
    slot = (holes
        .workplane(offset=-thickness/2)
        .moveTo(-slot_width/2, -slot_length/2)
        .lineTo(slot_width/2, -slot_length/2)
        .threePointArc((slot_width/2, -slot_length/2), (slot_width/2, slot_length/2))
        .lineTo(slot_width/2, slot_length/2)
        .threePointArc((-slot_width/2, slot_length/2), (-slot_width/2, -slot_length/2))
        .close()
        .extrude(thickness))
    
    # Combine all features
    result = slot.cut(profile)
    
    return result

result = generate_model()

import cadquery as cq
from cadquery import Vector

def create_model():
    # Define parameters
    plate_length = 200.0
    plate_width = 100.0
    plate_thickness = 5.0
    cutout_width = 80.0
    cutout_height = 40.0
    hole_diameter_small = 3.0
    hole_diameter_large = 6.0
    corner_radius = 2.0
    
    # Create the base plate with rounded corners
    plate = (cq.Workplane(cq.Plane(cq.Vector(0, 0, 0)))
        .rect(plate_length, plate_width)
        .fillet(corner_radius)
        .extrude(-plate_thickness))
    
    # Cut out the rectangular opening
    cutout = (cq.Workplane(cq.Plane(cq.Vector(0, 0, plate_thickness/2)))
        .moveTo((plate_length - cutout_width)/2, (plate_width - cutout_height)/2)
        .lineTo((plate_length + cutout_width)/2, (plate_width - cutout_height)/2)
        .lineTo((plate_length + cutout_width)/2, (plate_width + cutout_height)/2)
        .lineTo((plate_length - cutout_width)/2, (plate_width + cutout_height)/2)
        .close()
        .extrude(-plate_thickness))
    
    plate = plate.cut(cutout)
    
    # Add mounting holes on each side
    # Top side (smaller holes)
    top_holes = [
        ((plate_length - cutout_width)/2 - 15, (plate_width + cutout_height)/2 - 15),
        ((plate_length - cutout_width)/2 + 15, (plate_width + cutout_height)/2 - 15),
        ((plate_length + cutout_width)/2 - 15, (plate_width + cutout_height)/2 - 15),
        ((plate_length + cutout_width)/2 + 15, (plate_width + cutout_height)/2 - 15),
    ]
    
    # Bottom side (one large hole and three smaller ones)
    bottom_holes = [
        ((plate_length - cutout_width)/2 - 15, (plate_width - cutout_height)/2 + 15),
        ((plate_length - cutout_width)/2 + 15, (plate_width - cutout_height)/2 + 15),
        ((plate_length + cutout_width)/2 - 15, (plate_width - cutout_height)/2 + 15),
        ((plate_length + cutout_width)/2 + 15, (plate_width - cutout_height)/2 + 15),
    ]
    
    # Create holes for top side
    for x, y in top_holes:
        plate = plate.hole(x, y, hole_diameter_small)
    
    # Create holes for bottom side
    for x, y in bottom_holes:
        if abs(y - (plate_width - cutout_height)/2) < 10:
            plate = plate.hole(x, y, hole_diameter_large)
        else:
            plate = plate.hole(x, y, hole_diameter_small)
    
    return plate

result = create_model()

import cadquery as cq
from cadquery import Vector

def generate_model():
    # Define parametric dimensions
    length = 100.0
    width = 20.0
    height = 15.0
    
    # Create a hexagonal prism by rotating and extruding a rectangle
    # Start with a rectangle that will be rotated to form the hexagon
    rect = cq.Workplane(cq.Plane())
    rect = rect.moveTo(-width/2, -height/2)
    rect = rect.lineTo(width/2, -height/2)
    rect = rect.lineTo(width/2, height/2)
    rect = rect.lineTo(-width/2, height/2)
    rect = rect.close()
    
    # Rotate the rectangle around the Z axis to create a hexagonal shape
    # We need to rotate it so that the corners align properly for a hexagon
    # A regular hexagon can be approximated by rotating a rectangle by 30 degrees
    rotated_rect = rect.rotate(30, (0, 0, 0))
    
    # Extrude the rotated rectangle along the X axis to create the prism
    prism = rotated_rect.extrude(length)
    
    # Move the prism to the center of the coordinate system
    centered_prism = prism.translate((length/2, 0, 0))
    
    result = centered_prism

if __name__ == "__main__":
    result = generate_model()

import cadquery as cq
from cadquery import Vector

def create_model():
    # Define parameters for the model
    thickness = 10.0
    radius_main = 80.0
    radius_small = 30.0
    hole_radius = 15.0
    offset_x = 20.0
    
    # Create the base workplane
    wp = cq.Workplane(cq.Plane())
    
    # Draw the main body with rounded corners using a path
    path_coords = [
        (offset_x, -radius_small),
        (radius_main + offset_x, -radius_small),
        (radius_main + offset_x, radius_small),
        (offset_x, radius_small)
    ]
    
    # Create a smooth curve between points
    spline_path = cq.Spline(path_coords, degree=3, tension=0.5)
    
    # Add the outer profile to the workplane
    profile = wp.moveTo(offset_x, -radius_small).splineTo(
        radius_main + offset_x, -radius_small,
        radius_main + offset_x, radius_small,
        offset_x, radius_small,
        periodic=False
    )
    
    # Extrude the profile to create the solid body
    body = profile.extrude(thickness)
    
    # Create the circular hole in the center
    hole = cq.Workplane(cq.Plane(body.faces().filterByType('FACE').first().normalAt()))
    hole = hole.circle(hole_radius).extrude(-thickness)
    
    # Subtract the hole from the main body
    result = body.cut(hole)
    
    return result

# Generate the final model
result = create_model()

def generate_model():
    # Define parametric dimensions
    width = 100.0
    height = 200.0
    thickness = 10.0
    
    # Create the L-shaped profile in the XY plane
    l_profile = cq.Workplane(cq.Plane())
    
    # Start at origin, move along X axis by width
    l_profile.moveTo(0, 0)
    l_profile.lineTo(width, 0)
    
    # Move up by thickness (bottom flange)
    l_profile.lineTo(width, thickness)
    
    # Move left by (width - thickness) to start vertical part
    l_profile.lineTo(thickness, thickness)
    
    # Move up by (height - thickness)
    l_profile.lineTo(thickness, height)
    
    # Close back to origin
    l_profile.lineTo(0, height)
    l_profile.close()
    
    # Extrude the profile to create the 3D object
    result = l_profile.extrude(thickness)
    
    return result

import cadquery as cq
from cadquery import Vector

def generate_model():
    # Define parameters for the clamp bracket
    wall_thickness = 10.0
    inner_radius = 40.0
    outer_radius = 50.0
    handle_length = 80.0
    handle_width = 30.0
    hole_diameter = 12.0
    
    # Create the main curved body (a torus-like shape with a flat side)
    # We'll build this by creating a profile and sweeping it, or using boolean operations
    
    # Start with a rectangular block that will become the base of the clamp
    base_block = cq.Workplane(cq.Plane(cq.Vector(0, 0, 0)))
    
    # Create the profile for the clamp's cross-section
    profile = base_block.moveTo(-outer_radius, -wall_thickness/2) \
                      .lineTo(inner_radius, -wall_thickness/2) \
                      .lineTo(inner_radius, wall_thickness/2) \
                      .lineTo(-outer_radius, wall_thickness/2) \
                      .close()
    
    # Extrude the profile to create the curved section
    curved_section = profile.extrude(handle_length)
    
    # Now we need to add the mounting tab with the hole
    # First, let's create a separate block for the tab
    tab_block = cq.Workplane(cq.Plane(cq.Vector(0, 0, 0)))
    
    # Position the tab at the end of the curved section
    tab_pos = cq.Vector(-handle_length + wall_thickness/2, 0, 0)
    tab_plane = cq.Plane(tab_pos, cq.Vector(0, 0, 1))
    
    # Create the tab profile
    tab_profile = tab_block.moveTo(wall_thickness/2, -wall_thickness/2) \
                           .lineTo(wall_thickness/2, wall_thickness/2) \
                           .lineTo(-wall_thickness/2, wall_thickness/2) \
                           .lineTo(-wall_thickness/2, -wall_thickness/2) \
                           .close()
    
    # Extrude the tab
    tab = tab_profile.extrude(wall_thickness)
    
    # Move the tab to its final position
    tab = tab.moved(tab_pos)
    
    # Combine the curved section and the tab
    combined = curved_section.union(tab)
    
    # Add the mounting hole in the tab
    hole_position = cq.Vector(-handle_length + wall_thickness/2, 0, 0)
    hole = cq.Workplane(cq.Plane(hole_position, cq.Vector(0, 0, 1)))
    hole = hole.moveTo(0, 0).circle(hole_diameter/2).extrude(-wall_thickness)
    
    # Subtract the hole from the combined part
    result = combined.cut(hole)
    
    return result

result = generate_model()

from cadquery import Vector

def generate_model():
    # Define parametric dimensions
    length = 150.0   # Length of the block
    width = 60.0     # Width of the block
    height = 20.0    # Height/Thickness of the block
    hole_diameter = 8.0   # Diameter of the holes
    hole_depth = 5.0      # Depth of the holes
    hole_spacing = 30.0   # Spacing between centers of adjacent holes
    
    # Create the main rectangular block
    block = cq.Workplane(cq.Plane())
    block.box(length, width, height)
    
    # Calculate positions for the three holes along the length
    # We'll place them symmetrically with equal spacing
    total_hole_length = (hole_spacing * 2) + hole_diameter
    offset_from_edge = (length - total_hole_length) / 2
    
    # Create holes by cutting cylinders
    hole_positions = [
        Vector(offset_from_edge, width / 2, 0),
        Vector(offset_from_edge + hole_spacing, width / 2, 0),
        Vector(offset_from_edge + hole_spacing * 2, width / 2, 0)
    ]
    
    # Cut each hole from the block
    result = block
    for pos in hole_positions:
        hole = cq.Workplane(cq.Plane(pos))
        hole.cylinder(hole_diameter / 2, hole_depth)
        result = result.cut(hole)
    
    return result

def generate_model():

    slot = slot.moveTo(0, 0).lineTo(slot_width, 0).lineTo(slot_width, slot_depth).lineTo(0, slot_depth).close()
    slot = slot.extrude(-thickness)
    
    # Subtract the slot from the base plate
    result = base_plate.cut(slot)
    
    return result

from cadquery import Vector

def generate_model():
    # Define parametric dimensions
    outer_radius = 10.0
    inner_radius = 4.0
    length = 30.0
    angle_start = -60.0  # degrees
    angle_end = 60.0     # degrees

    # Create a circle profile for the outer shape
    outer_profile = cq.Workplane(cq.Plane())
    outer_profile.moveTo(outer_radius, 0)
    outer_profile.arc(angle_start, angle_end, outer_radius)
    outer_profile.lineTo(-outer_radius, 0)
    outer_profile.close()

    # Create a circle profile for the inner shape (offset by inner_radius)
    inner_profile = cq.Workplane(cq.Plane())
    inner_profile.moveTo(inner_radius, 0)
    inner_profile.arc(angle_start, angle_end, inner_radius)
    inner_profile.lineTo(-inner_radius, 0)
    inner_profile.close()

    # Extrude both profiles along the Z-axis to create the solid
    outer_solid = outer_profile.extrude(length)
    inner_solid = inner_profile.extrude(length)

    # Subtract the inner solid from the outer solid to create the final geometry
    result = outer_solid.cut(inner_solid)

    return result

from cadquery import Vector

def generate_model():
    # Define parametric dimensions
    length = 200.0
    height = 150.0
    width = 80.0
    thickness = 10.0
    chamfer_radius = 3.0
    
    # Create base rectangle for the profile (side view)
    profile = cq.Workplane(cq.Plane())
    profile.moveTo(0, 0)
    profile.lineTo(length, 0)
    profile.lineTo(length, height)
    profile.lineTo(0, height)
    profile.close()
    
    # Extrude to create the main body
    body = profile.extrude(thickness)
    
    # Create a second profile that will be subtracted to form the U-shape
    cut_profile = cq.Workplane(cq.Plane())
    cut_profile.moveTo(thickness/2, thickness/2)
    cut_profile.lineTo(length - thickness/2, thickness/2)
    cut_profile.lineTo(length - thickness/2, height - thickness/2)
    cut_profile.lineTo(thickness/2, height - thickness/2)
    cut_profile.close()
    
    # Extrude the cut profile to create the void
    cut_body = cut_profile.extrude(thickness)
    
    # Subtract the cut body from the main body
    result = body.cut(cut_body)
    
    # Apply fillets to all edges with radius
    result = result.edges().filter(lambda e: e.tag == 'Edge').fillet(chamfer_radius)
    
    return result

import cadquery as cq
from cadquery import Vector

def generate_model():
    # Define parameters for the part
    plate_length = 150.0
    plate_width = 100.0
    plate_thickness = 10.0
    corner_radius = 15.0
    cutout_depth = 30.0
    cutout_width = 20.0
    large_hole_diameter = 30.0
    small_hole_diameter = 8.0
    
    # Create the base plate with rounded corners and fillets
    plate = (cq.Workplane("XY")
        .moveTo(-plate_length/2, -plate_width/2)
        .threePointArc((-plate_length/2 + corner_radius), -plate_width/2)
        .lineTo(plate_length/2, -plate_width/2)
        .threePointArc((plate_length/2 - corner_radius), -plate_width/2)
        .lineTo(plate_length/2, plate_width/2)
        .threePointArc((plate_length/2 - corner_radius), plate_width/2)
        .lineTo(-plate_length/2, plate_width/2)
        .threePointArc((-plate_length/2 + corner_radius), plate_width/2)
        .close()
        .extrude(plate_thickness))
    
    # Add the U-shaped cutout on the right side
    cutout_profile = (cq.Workplane("XZ")
        .moveTo(cutout_depth, 0)
        .lineTo(cutout_depth, -cutout_width/2)
        .threePointArc(cutout_depth - corner_radius, -cutout_width/2)
        .lineTo(cutout_depth, cutout_width/2)
        .threePointArc(cutout_depth - corner_radius, cutout_width/2)
        .lineTo(0, cutout_width/2)
        .threePointArc(-corner_radius, cutout_width/2)
        .lineTo(0, -cutout_width/2)
        .threePointArc(-corner_radius, -cutout_width/2)
        .close()
        .extrude(-plate_length))
    
    # Subtract the cutout from the plate
    plate = plate.cut(cutout_profile)
    
    # Add the large central hole
    large_hole = (cq.Workplane("XY")
        .circle(large_hole_diameter/2)
        .workplane(offset=plate_length/4))
    
    plate = plate.cut(large_hole.extrude(plate_thickness + 1))
    
    # Add the smaller holes at specific positions
    small_holes_positions = [
        (-plate_length/4, -plate_width/6),
        (-plate_length/4, plate_width/6),
        (plate_length/4, -plate_width/3),
        (plate_length/4, plate_width/3),
        (plate_length/4, 0),
        (plate_length/2, -plate_width/4),
        (plate_length/2, plate_width/4),
        (plate_length/2, 0),
        (plate_length/2, -plate_width/8),
        (plate_length/2, plate_width/8),
        (plate_length/2, -plate_width/16),
        (plate_length/2, plate_width/16)
    ]
    
    for pos in small_holes_positions:
        hole = (cq.Workplane("XY")
            .circle(small_hole_diameter/2)
            .moveTo(pos[0], pos[1]))
        
        plate = plate.cut(hole.extrude(plate_thickness + 1))
    
    return plate

result = generate_model()

def generate_model():
    # Define parametric dimensions for the part
    length = 10.0
    width = 8.0
    height = 6.0
    cutout_width = 3.5
    cutout_depth = 4.0
    
    # Start with a base rectangular prism
    solid = (cq.Workplane()
        .moveTo(0, 0)
        .lineTo(length, 0)
        .lineTo(length, width)
        .lineTo(0, width)
        .close()
        .extrude(height))
    
    # Create the main body by extruding the profile
    profile = (cq.Workplane()
        .moveTo(0, 0)
        .lineTo(length, 0)
        .lineTo(length, width)
        .lineTo(0, width)
        .close())
    
    # Extrude the profile to create the main block
    main_block = profile.extrude(height)
    
    # Add the vertical support structure
    support_profile = (cq.Workplane()
        .moveTo(0, 0)
        .lineTo(length/2, 0)
        .lineTo(length/2, width)
        .lineTo(0, width)
        .close())
    
    support_height = height * 0.7
    support = support_profile.extrude(support_height)
    
    # Combine the main block and support
    combined = main_block.union(support)
    
    # Create the cutout on the front face
    cutout_profile = (cq.Workplane()
        .moveTo(cutout_depth, 0)
        .threePointArc((cutout_depth + cutout_width/2, 0), 
                       (length - cutout_depth, 0))
        .threePointArc((length - cutout_depth, 0), 
                       (length - cutout_depth, cutout_width))
        .threePointArc((length - cutout_depth + cutout_width/2, cutout_width), 
                       (cutout_depth, cutout_width))
        .close())
    
    # Extrude the cutout in the negative Z direction
    cutout = cutout_profile.extrude(-height)
    
    # Subtract the cutout from the combined geometry
    result = combined.cut(cutout)
    
    return result

# Define parameters for the model
L = 100.0  # Length of the main body

H = 20.0   # Height of the main body
D1 = 10.0  # Diameter of the first hole
D2 = 8.0   # Diameter of the second hole
P1 = 30.0  # Pitch between holes
R = 5.0    # Radius of the cutout
CUT_W = 15.0  # Width of the cutout
CUT_D = 10.0  # Depth of the cutout

# Create the base block
base = cq.Workplane(cq.Plane()) \
    .box(L, W, H)

# Add the two through-holes on the side face
side_face = cq.Workplane(cq.Plane(base.vertices()[0].position)) \
    .add(base.faces().filter(lambda f: abs(f.normalAt().z) < 0.1)[0])

hole_pos1 = (L/2 - P1/2, D1/2)
hole_pos2 = (L/2 + P1/2, D1/2)

side_face = side_face \
    .circle(D1/2).moveTo(hole_pos1) \
    .extrude(-H) \
    .circle(D2/2).moveTo(hole_pos2) \
    .extrude(-H)

# Add the cutout feature at one end
cutout_plane = cq.Plane(Vector(0, 0, 0), Vector(0, 0, 1))
cutout_profile = cq.Workplane(cutout_plane) \
    .rect(W/2, CUT_W) \
    .circle(R) \
    .offset(CUT_W/2)

cutout_shape = cutout_profile.extrude(CUT_D)
cutout_position = cq.Vector(0, -W/2, 0)
cutout_moved = cutout_shape.moved(cutout_position)

# Combine all features into final geometry
result = base.cut(side_face).cut(cutout_moved)

def generate_model():

    length = 200.0  # mm
    width = 100.0   # mm
    height = 50.0   # mm
    wall_thickness = 10.0  # mm
    
    # Create the outer box
    outer_box = cq.Workplane(cq.Plane())
    outer_box = outer_box.moveTo(0, 0)
    outer_box = outer_box.lineTo(length, 0)
    outer_box = outer_box.lineTo(length, width)
    outer_box = outer_box.lineTo(0, width)
    outer_box = outer_box.close()
    outer_box = outer_box.extrude(height)
    
    # Create the inner void by offsetting the top face inward
    inner_void = outer_box.faces().filter(lambda f: f.normalAt().dot(cq.Vector(0, 0, 1)) > 0)[0]
    inner_void_pos = inner_void.positionAt(0).positionAt(0).positionAt(0)
    
    # Calculate inner dimensions
    inner_length = length - 2 * wall_thickness
    inner_width = width - 2 * wall_thickness
    inner_height = height - wall_thickness
    
    # Create the inner rectangle on the top face
    inner_rect = outer_box.faces().filter(lambda f: f.normalAt().dot(cq.Vector(0, 0, 1)) > 0)[0].workplane()
    inner_rect = inner_rect.moveTo(wall_thickness, wall_thickness)
    inner_rect = inner_rect.lineTo(inner_length, wall_thickness)
    inner_rect = inner_rect.lineTo(inner_length, inner_width)
    inner_rect = inner_rect.lineTo(wall_thickness, inner_width)
    inner_rect = inner_rect.close()
    
    # Cut the inner void through the entire height
    cut_feature = inner_rect.extrude(-height)
    
    # Combine to create the hollow box
    result = outer_box.cut(cut_feature)
    
    return result
